operating_state: Net earnings attributable to common stockholders increased to $584 million from $463 million, with Core FFO rising to $1.222 billion from $1.157 billion driven by rental revenue growth to $1.828 billion. Strong rent change of 67.6% on lease rollovers and same-store NOI growth of 4.1% net effective supported performance across the 636 million square feet consolidated operating portfolio maintained at 97.0% occupancy.
cost_structure: Real Estate Segment NOI expanded to $1.362 billion from $1.214 billion, while Strategic Capital Segment NOI decreased to $50 million from $63 million. Interest payments net of capitalized amounts increased significantly to $211 million from $130 million reflecting higher debt levels and rising rates. General and administrative expenses rose with depreciation and amortization increasing to $638 million from $602 million due to asset base growth from the June 2023 acquisition.
material_events: Issued $1.9 billion of senior notes with 4.8% weighted average interest rate and 13-year maturity, including $1.25 billion in January and C$550 million notes due 2029. Established commercial paper program and extinguished $500 million U.S. dollar term loan in February. Generated $285 million net proceeds from property contributions and sales with $57 million in net gains. Quarterly common stock dividend increased to $0.96 per share from $0.87.
macro_exposures: Management expects customer leasing decision delays over the next several quarters due to economic and geopolitical uncertainty. Foreign currency exposure across British pound sterling, Canadian dollar, euro, and Japanese yen investments partially mitigated through functional currency borrowings and derivative instruments, with unrealized gains of $35 million versus prior period losses of $8 million.
forward_outlook: Development pipeline includes 121 properties with $7.5 billion total expected investment, 39.3% leased with $3.1 billion remaining to be spent through August 2026. Estimated lease mark-to-market of approximately 50% provides organic growth runway from in-place rent rollovers. Land bank supports potential $34.2 billion of future development across global markets.
uncertainty_profile: Remaining equity commitments of $347 million to co-investment ventures with partner commitments of $2.3 billion through 2033. Development completion and leasing execution risk on $4.4 billion invested portfolio. Debt refinancing requirements with $107 million maturing in remainder of 2024 and $434 million in 2025.
